mnemonics: SPACE DIMS: Space Partitioning And Clustering Divides Indexed Multi-dimensional Space
keyPoints: Multi-dimensional indexing structures, like R-trees and k-d trees, efficiently organize data based on multiple attributes (e.g., spatial coordinates).
These structures enable fast range queries and nearest neighbor searches by partitioning the data space hierarchically.
The choice of structure depends on the data's dimensionality and the specific type of queries (e.g., spatial vs. high-dimensional feature vectors).
commonMistakes: Assuming a standard B-tree is efficient for multi-dimensional range queries, when it is designed for single-dimensional data.
Forgetting that the 'curse of dimensionality' can make these indexes less effective for a very high number of dimensions.
